title: Enabling Customer-Centricity
subtitle: Leading organizational transformation at Sweden's largest electricity distributor to embed customer-first thinking across operations
challenge: The client, one of Sweden’s largest electricity distributors with 900,000 customers. High support costs and limited self-service capabilities created friction in customer interactions, reinforced by operations-focused processes and culture. This hurt organizational efficiency, caused unnecessary cost, and harmed overall brand experience. With rising electricity prices and increasing customer expectations, a fundamental shift toward a more coordinated, customer-centric approach was crucial.

designProcess.intro: This was a transformation project spanning organizational alignment, customer research, team scaling, and capability building. My work moved from understanding the current state through defining the customer lifecycle to embedding new ways of working across the organization.

steps.title: Understanding Client Needs
steps.description: I conducted extensive discovery interviews and stakeholder workshops across operations, support, product, and leadership teams. Mapped organizational structures, identified silos, and built consensus around the need for customer-centric transformation.

steps.title: Long term strategy
steps.description: In my delivery, I developed and delivered a comprehensive long term strategy for customer journey mapping and built organizational capability by expanding the team. Facilitated cross-functional workshops to foster a customer-centric, outcome over output mindset, across the company.

steps.title: Research & Journey Production
steps.description: As part of my process in producing customer journeys, I conducted contextual interviews with both internal stakeholders and real customers. Iteratively drafted and validated customer journey assumptions, mapping needs, expectations, pains, and gains.

insights: Organizational transformation requires alignment before implementation — research and strategy phases were as important as produced journey maps
Team scaling through evidence and quick wins was essential — one designer couldn't deliver organizational scope, but early credibility enabled investment
Customer journey maps only created value when connected to operations — insights-to-action translation was the critical bridge
Leading without formal authority meant influence through evidence, consistent communication, and demonstrated business impact

deliverables.intro: I delivered a comprehensive B2C/B2B customer lifecycle framework, detailed customer journeys with integrated business process views, and embedded design methodologies across the organization. Besides growing the team from two to seven designers, a standout deliverable was the solar panel customer experience work package which achieved a 52% Customer Effort Score improvement, validating my approach.

images.alt: B2C/B2B customer lifecycle framework — full end-to-end view
Solar panel customer journey map showing experience improvements

results.intro: The transformation delivered measurable improvements across organizational and customer metrics.

kpis.label: Organizational adoption
kpis.value: Customer-centric team KPIs promoted to company wide KPIs

kpis.label: Launched team merging
kpis.value: The CX and product team were merged to improve collaboration
